coding_notes: Verdict vocabulary: affirm = vendor asserts the canonical/dominant answer; disagree = vendor addresses the claim but lands on a different number/identity (record the alternative in 'alternative'); ignore = vendor did not address the sub-claim; refusal = vendor refused the prompt entirely. For numerical claims, two vendors AGREE if their numbers fall within a tolerance noted in the claim's 'tolerance' field; otherwise disagree.
ground_truth_notes: PTS1 of A. flavus uricase (UniProt Q00511) is SKL at the C-terminus (residues 299-301 of 301 aa mature protein, per Legoux et al. 1992 JBC and confirmed in UniProt). Km values from primary literature: ~35 uM (Laboureur & Langlois 1968 native enzyme); 10 uM (Kahn & Tipton 1997 biochemistry); 28 uM (Conley & Priest 1980, A. niger but commonly cited). Variation reflects assay-condition differences (pH, buffer, temperature).

claims.claim_text: Native quaternary structure is a homotetramer.
Subunit molecular weight ~34 kDa, native MW ~135 kDa (tolerance: ±5 kDa).
No metal or organic cofactor required (cofactor-independent oxidase).
Enzyme-catalyzed step produces 5-hydroxyisourate + H2O2 (with non-enzymatic decarboxylation to allantoin + CO2).

deepseek.value_quoted: uric acid + O2 + H2O -> 5-hydroxyisourate + H2O2 (5-HIU decarboxylates non-enzymatically to allantoin + CO2)

gemini.value_quoted: Uric acid + O2 + 2H2O -> Allantoin + H2O2 + CO2 (only overall balanced, does not name 5-HIU intermediate)

openai.value_quoted: urate + O2 + H2O -> 5-hydroxyisourate + H2O2; in aqueous solution converts to allantoin + CO2

claims.claim_text: Peroxisomal targeting signal is SKL at the extreme C-terminus (residues 299-301 of the 301-aa mature protein). GROUND TRUTH per Legoux et al. 1992 + UniProt Q00511.
Published Km for uric acid (any specific value with primary source). Variation across the published range (10-60 uM) is acceptable; disagreement flagged when vendors quote incompatible single values.

openai.value_quoted: explicitly declined: 'uncertain from primary recall', low-tens-of-uM range only
